sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check the player position after 5 RIGHT actions. The player should be at cols 44-48 now.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check player position
after = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in after:
    if n['id'] == 6:
        print(f"Player boundary: {n['boundary']}")
    if n['id'] == 7:
        print(f"Red path pixels: {n['pixels']}")

# Check the area around player
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nPlayer area (rows 7-15, cols 40-52):")
for i in range(7, 16):
    print(f"  {i}: {ascii_lines[i][40:52]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The player boundary is very strange - it spans the entire maze. This doesn't look right. The player seems to be at cols 40-44 based on the boundary [[7, 13], [7, 43], [13, 43], [13, 42], ...]. Wait, that doesn't make sense. Let me look at the actual player position from the ASCII.
Looking at rows 7-15, cols 40-52:
- Row 7: BBBBWWWWWWWW (W at cols 44-51)
- Row 8: bbbBWWWWWWWW (b at cols 40-42, W at cols 44-51)
- Row 9: bbbBWWWWWWWW (b at cols 40-42, W at cols 44-51)
- Row 10: BbbBWWWWWWWW (b at cols 41-43, W at cols 44-51)
- Row 11: bbbBWWWWWWWW (b at cols 40-42, W at cols 44-51)
- Row 12: bbbBWWWWWWWW (b at cols 40-42, W at cols 44-51)
- Row 13: RBBBWWWWWWWW (R at col 40, W at cols 44-51)
So the player (blue 'b') is at cols 40-43, rows 8-12. The white corridor is at cols 44-51. The player is just to the left of the white corridor.
Wait, but the player boundary shows [[7, 13], [7, 43], [13, 43], ...] which spans the entire maze. This is confusing. Let me look at the segmentation more carefully.
Actually, looking at the boundary, it seems like the player node (id=6) has a very complex boundary that traces the entire maze perimeter. This might be because the segmentation is grouping the player with the maze walls or something.
Let me focus on the ASCII output instead. The player (blue 'b') is at:
- Row 8: cols 40-42 (bbb)
- Row 9: cols 40-42 (bbb)
- Row 10: cols 41-43 (bbB - wait, the B is at col 43)
- Row 11: cols 40-42 (bbb)
- Row 12: cols 40-42 (bbb)
So the player is at cols 40-43, rows 8-12. The white corridor starts at col 44. The player is adjacent to the white corridor.
Now, the red path (R) is at col 40, rows 13+. The player is at col 40-43, which overlaps with the red path's column.
Let me try DOWN to see if the player can enter the white corridor below.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player is at cols 40-43, rows 8-12. White corridor is at cols 44+.
# Let me try DOWN to see if the player can move down
action(['DOWN'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
